question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCallRequest with gas <= 10,000 (e.g., gas=1) so that scaleGas() returns 0 and gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) is never called, allowing unlimited transactions to bypass the gas-per-second throttle and gossip an unbounded number of transactions per second?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: scaleGas()] Can an unprivileged external user set gas=10000 (exactly GAS_SCALE_FACTOR) so that scaleGas() returns 0, causing gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) to always succeed, effectively making the gas throttle a no-op and allowing the attacker to gossip transactions at the full requestsPerSecond rate without any gas budget deduction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger restore() with an arbitrarily large gas value (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE) to overflow or massively overfill the gasLimitBucket with tokens, effectively resetting the gas throttle and allowing subsequent transactions to bypass the gas-per-second limit and gossip freely?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential check order in throttle()—where rateLimitBucket.tryConsume(1) is consumed before gasLimitBucket is checked—to drain the rateLimitBucket tokens without consuming any gas tokens (by sending gas=0 requests), starving legitimate users from the rate limit while the attacker's gas budget remains untouched?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gas just above 10,000 (e.g., gas=10,001) so that scaleGas() returns 1, consuming only 1 gas token per request, and then flood the system with up to gasPerSecond/10000 requests per second, effectively multiplying their transaction gossip rate by a factor of 10,000 compared to honest high-gas users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: test()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the AtomicLong counter in RequestProperties to overflow from Long.MAX_VALUE to Long.MIN_VALUE after submitting Long.MAX_VALUE+1 requests, causing counter.getAndIncrement() >= limit to evaluate as false (since Long.MIN_VALUE < limit), thereby bypassing the per-filter request limit and re-enabling REJECT or THROTTLE actions that were supposed to be permanently disabled?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: test()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the empty-filters fallback in RequestProperties.test() (return filters.isEmpty()) to ensure every request matches a THROTTLE or REJECT rule when no filters are configured, or conversely, craft requests that always match an empty filter list to trigger unintended LOG actions that flood application logs and mask real attack traffic?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass a REJECT filter by crafting a ContractCallRequest whose field values (DATA, FROM, TO, BLOCK, GAS, VALUE, ESTIMATE) do not match any configured RequestFilter expression, causing all filter.test() calls to return false and the action() method to never be invoked, allowing the request to pass through and gossip a transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: test()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the probabilistic rate check (RandomUtils.secure().randomLong(0L, 100L) >= rate) for LOG and REJECT actions to statistically bypass REJECT filters by sending many requests, knowing that on average (100-rate)% of requests will not be rejected, allowing a fraction of malicious transactions to be gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-user throttling in the global rateLimitBucket and gasLimitBucket to consume the entire shared request quota, preventing all other users from gossiping any transactions (denial-of-service against legitimate gossip)?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger restore() repeatedly with gas values just above GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (e.g., gas=10,001) to add 1 token per call to the gasLimitBucket, and by calling restore() in a tight loop, accumulate enough tokens to allow a burst of high-gas transactions to be gossiped beyond the configured gasPerSecond limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a ContractCallRequest with a DATA field containing a substring that matches a CONTAINS filter expression (e.g., embedding a known function selector in arbitrary calldata) to intentionally trigger a LOG action, flooding the application logs with attacker-controlled content and obscuring legitimate transaction gossip activity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: test()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the case-insensitive CONTAINS filter (Strings.CI::contains) by submitting DATA values with mixed-case hex strings (e.g., '0xDeAdBeEf' vs '0xdeadbeef') to bypass a REJECT filter that uses CONTAINS matching, allowing the transaction to be gossiped when the filter was intended to block it?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: test()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the case-insensitive EQUALS filter (String::equalsIgnoreCase) on the FROM or TO field to bypass address-based REJECT rules by submitting addresses with different casing (e.g., '0xABCD...' vs '0xabcd...'), allowing blocked addresses to gossip transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of requests that each pass the rateLimitBucket check but fail the gasLimitBucket check, causing the rateLimitBucket to be drained without any gas being consumed, effectively locking out all users from the rate limit while the attacker can still gossip low-gas transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttleOpcodeRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the opcodeRateLimitBucket by sending rapid opcode requests, exhausting the opcodeRequestsPerSecond limit (default=1), and thereby preventing any legitimate opcode-based transaction gossip while the attacker's own non-opcode transactions continue unimpeded?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the global gasLimitBucket is shared across all concurrent requests (no synchronization beyond bucket4j internals) to race-condition the bucket consumption, where two concurrent requests each see sufficient tokens but together consume more than the limit, allowing more transactions to be gossiped than the gas budget permits?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: restore()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger restore() with gas=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing scaleGas(Long.MAX_VALUE) to return Long.MAX_VALUE/10000 = 922337203685477, and then gasLimitBucket.addTokens() to add an astronomically large number of tokens, effectively disabling the gas throttle for all subsequent transactions and allowing unlimited gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCallRequest with gas=0 so that scaleGas(0) returns 0, and gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) is a no-op (consuming nothing), allowing the attacker to gossip transactions at the full requestsPerSecond rate with zero gas cost to the throttle budget?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: action()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that matches a LOG-action filter, causing log.info('{}', request) to log the full ContractCallRequest object, and by embedding newline characters or log-injection payloads in the DATA or FROM fields, forge fake log entries that mislead operators about transaction gossip activity?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the OR logic in RequestProperties.test() (first matching filter returns true) to craft a request that matches only a benign LOG filter but not a REJECT filter in the same RequestProperties, allowing the transaction to be logged but not rejected, and thus gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: test()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RequestProperties.test() evaluates filters with OR semantics (returns true on first match) to bypass a multi-filter REJECT rule by ensuring none of the individual filter conditions match their request fields, allowing the transaction to pass all filters and be gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request deduplication in throttle() to submit the same transaction multiple times within the same second, consuming rate and gas tokens for each duplicate, and if restore() is called for each, effectively recycling the gas budget to gossip the same transaction repeatedly?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: scaleGas()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the floor division in scaleGas() (Math.floorDiv(gas, 10000)) to submit gas values just below multiples of 10,000 (e.g., gas=19,999 returns 1 instead of 2), effectively halving the gas cost to the throttle and doubling the number of transactions that can be gossiped per second?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManager.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user send a burst of requests at the start of each second (when buckets are refilled) to consume the entire requestsPerSecond quota in a single burst, preventing any other user from gossiping transactions for the remainder of that second?
